The species Methonica doniana is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Within the plant kingdom, Methonica doniana is further classified as a dicotyledon, meaning it has two cotyledons or seed leaves. This classification is based on the plant's anatomy and reproductive structures.

Its extracts have been studied for their antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including wounds, infections, and respiratory problems.
